mapping.a_element: Method of Maximum (relative) Entropy — maximize entropy subject to constraints to select the least-biased distribution
mapping.b_element: Jaynes' information theory (MaxEnt) formalism applied to non-equilibrium stationary states

mapping.a_element: moment constraints (and observed data) imposed on the inference
mapping.b_element: macroscopic/flux constraints on the open, flux-driven system in the slowly-driven limit

mapping.a_element: canonical posterior probability distribution over outcomes (multinomial die tosses)
mapping.b_element: constructed probability distribution over microscopic phase-space trajectories

mapping.a_element: Lagrange-multiplier canonical (exponential-family) form of the updated distribution
mapping.b_element: canonical trajectory distribution from which the fluctuation theorem, MaxEnt production, and SOC are derived

brief_justification: Both are instances of Jaynes' Maximum Entropy inference: A maximizes relative entropy subject to moment/data constraints to obtain a canonical posterior over outcomes, while B maximizes (path) entropy subject to macroscopic flux constraints to construct the distribution over phase-space trajectories. The transferable machinery — maximize entropy under constraints via Lagrange multipliers to yield a canonical distribution — is identical; only the objects (outcomes vs. trajectories) and constraints (moments vs. fluxes) differ.
